title: EMDR and Trauma-Informed Care: An Overview
excerpt: How EMDR and related approaches support healing from difficult experiences.
author: Nayab Tahir
body:
**Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR)** is a structured therapy that helps the brain process traumatic memories so they become less emotionally overwhelming.

Trauma-informed psychotherapy recognizes that symptoms — anxiety, hypervigilance, relationship difficulties — are often adaptive responses to past experiences. Treatment prioritizes safety, pacing, and your agency.

At Voice Awareness, EMDR may be considered alongside brainspotting, IFS, and nervous-system-focused work when appropriate for your goals. A consultation helps determine what approach fits best.
